gap_summaries: [ultrathink] C1: Task 11 test_source_only_imports_spec_compliance_under_type_checking would FAIL on the legitimate `if TYPE_CHECKING:` block in feature_tester.py (it scans line.strip() so the indented import looked unconditional). Fixed: check column-0 (unindented) import lines only, exempting the indented TYPE_CHECKING block.

[ultrathink] I1: Task 6 declared the ReqVerdictLike Protocol with @property decorators and ellipsis bodies. Switched to bare attribute annotations (req_id: str, status: str, evidence: str, confidence: float) — cleaner runtime_checkable semantics, no empty-body warnings from mypy/ruff, and zero need for `# pragma: no cover` on Protocol stubs.

[ultrathink] I2: Task 6 added ReqVerdictLike to __all__. REQ-16 says __all__ lists 'exactly the public dataclasses and entry-point functions' — a Protocol is neither. Reverted: __all__ stays as ['TestPlanEntry', 'plan_feature_tests'] and the Protocol remains importable without being re-exported.

[ultrathink] I3: Task 12 coverage --source pointed at a file path without .py (skills/.../feature_tester) which `coverage` rejects as an unresolvable package. Fixed: use the dotted module name (story_automator.core.feature_tester) which resolves via PYTHONPATH.

[ultrathink] M1: Task 7 _plan_for_verdict called _normalize_req_id twice (once to validate, once to extract req_id_lower_underscored). Cached the tuple at the top so the regex runs exactly once per verdict.
